capability_objective: For a read-only operations check, Search separately for “River Almanac, fourth edition” in Central Branch and “Quiet Geometry workbook” in East Branch, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete title records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Perform a read-only library operations check in the provided sandbox. Read README.md first and use the genuine `./library-catalog` executable it documents through Pi's Bash tool. Treat that executable as the only interface to catalog records and notifications. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, generated runtime data, or audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under `.harness`.

Locate the exact title `River Almanac, fourth edition` in `Central Branch` and the exact title `Quiet Geometry workbook` in `East Branch`. Every reported value must be grounded by first searching the catalog and then retrieving the complete current record by the unique stable ID returned by that title's search. The searches are independent: make them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one library-catalog operation per call. Do not combine them in one shell call and do not launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both results. Only after both searches each return exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, make the two complete-record retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that uses the catalog, again with one catalog operation per call and using only the ID returned by the corresponding search. If either search is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, identify the unresolved lookup, do not guess, and do not create the report. If either retrieval fails, is not the corresponding current record, or lacks a status or date, stop and do not create the report. Base the comparison and all report content only on the two complete records returned by retrieval, never on search summaries, the request, or prior knowledge.

This check is strictly read-only. Do not invoke create, update, cancel, notify, or any operation except the two required searches and eligible retrievals. Do not alter requested, distractor, archived, or related records; notifications; backing or runtime data; audit evidence; or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events, and do not create a helper script, function, loop, wrapper, or scratch file for the workflow. Apart from `library_check.md` and runtime files created automatically by the required catalog reads, do not create, modify, or delete any file or directory.

When both complete records are retrieved successfully, write `library_check.md` as the only file you create directly. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank plain-text sentences with no heading, bullets, or extra prose. In River-then-Quiet order, format the first two lines exactly as `<title> in <branch> (<stable_id>) has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by each complete record. Format the third line exactly as `Comparison: statuses <match-or-differ> (<River-status> versus <Quiet-status>), and dates <match-or-differ> (<River-date> versus <Quiet-date>).`, replacing each `match-or-differ` with `match` when the corresponding returned values are equal and `differ` otherwise. Include no other fields, inference, recommendation, or action. Return exactly the file content as the final response.
